nativeRange.habitat: Trade cultivar selected from Rotala rotundifolia stock for intensified pink-to-magenta coloration; no independent wild range. The parent species occurs as a common weed of rice paddies, marshes, ditches, and the muddy margins of slow-moving streams across temperate and tropical Asia, growing both emersed (rounded leaves) and submersed (narrow lanceolate leaves often flushed wine-red under intense light).

decorPreferences: nutrient-rich aquasoil substrate with iron-rich root tabs
high light (PAR 50+) needed for the cultivar's signature pink-magenta coloration
CO2 injection generally recommended for compact form and intense color
soft, slightly acidic water is commonly reported to deepen the pink-magenta hue
regular trimming maintains bushy density and prevents legginess

summary: A selected cultivar of Rotala rotundifolia bred for intensified pink-to-magenta coloration. Carries the parent species' easy stem-plant growth habit, but commits more strongly to red top-growth under high light, CO2, and iron — a popular background or trimmed-bush midground plant in high-tech aquascapes.
careNotes:
Rotala 'Colorata' is a trade cultivar widely treated as a selected form of Rotala rotundifolia (parent entry fw-plant-046, Lythraceae, Myrtales — taxonomy and native-range data verified against Wikipedia's R. rotundifolia article). Care closely mirrors the parent species but the cultivar's signature coloration is generally reported to depend on high light (around 50+ PAR), CO2 injection, iron supplementation, and ideally low nitrate to push the deepest pink-magenta into the new growth. Under low light or without CO2, leaves tend to stay olive-green and the cultivar becomes visually indistinguishable from the standard 'Green' form. Soft acidic water (pH around 5.5-6.5) is commonly reported to intensify pink-magenta tones compared to the wider parent species' tolerance. Background stem placement is typical; trim regularly to maintain bushy density and to propagate via cuttings. Related entries: fw-plant-046 (Rotala rotundifolia parent), fw-plant-081 (Rotala indica), fw-plant-162 (Rotala macrandra), fw-plant-161 (Rotala H'Ra).

NOTE (dataStatus=needs_review): Only one source (Wikipedia, for the parent species Rotala rotundifolia) successfully confirmed taxonomy and habitat during this research pass. Cultivar-specific care values inherit from the well-researched parent entry plus the dispatch's special-notes guidance. Attempted sources that failed: Buce Plant product page (404 Not Found), Modern Aquarium index (no Colorata listing), Aquarium Co-Op plants collection (no Colorata listing), 2HR Aquarist comparison article (403 Forbidden), Tropica rotundifolia detail page (no usable content), aquariumbreeder.com Colorata-specific guide (404). Reviewer should verify the cultivar lineage and color-response specifics against Tropica, 2HR Aquarist, or a Buce Plant URL re-resolution.
breedingNotes: Propagated by stem cuttings — cut any portion of the stem and replant directly into the substrate, where roots and side shoots develop quickly. Pinching off the apical bud encourages the mother stem to throw multiple lateral shoots that can be trimmed and replanted for mass propagation. Floating cuttings will also root in the water column before planting.

primary.name: Wikipedia — Rotala rotundifolia
primary.notes: Used as primary fallback after the dispatch's intended primary (Buce Plant product page for 'Rotala rotundifolia Colorata') returned 404. Confirms parent-species binomial Rotala rotundifolia, family Lythraceae, order Myrtales, native range (India, China, Taiwan, Thailand, Laos, Vietnam), paddy/marsh habitat, dimorphic emersed-vs-submersed leaf form, and that intense light makes leaves 'almost wine red'. Does not specifically address the 'Colorata' cultivar.

additional.name: Dispatch baseline (parent species fw-plant-046)
additional.notes: Cultivar-specific water-parameter, lighting, CO2, and color-expression guidance inherited from the dispatch's special-notes block (high light + CO2 + iron required for color; reverts to olive-green without; soft acidic water deepens magenta). Not independently cross-verified during this research pass — flagged needs_review.
